data.body: DONE (config-loop, commit 479e4ac0). Renamed TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR -> REBAR_TRACKER_DIR (relocated/decoupled store is a supported feature, decision var 4). AC1 (REBAR_TRACKER_DIR honored everywhere; old name aliased + warned): new config.tracker_dir_override() returns REBAR_TRACKER_DIR (canonical) or the deprecated TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR with a WARN-ONCE deprecation message (_warn_once_legacy_env/_WARNED_LEGACY_ENV, cleared by reset_config_cache). All 6 env-read sites route through it: config.tracker_dir, reads.tracker_dir, bridge_fsck, reconciler inbound_translate, and the 3 _cli auto-init presence gates; the only remaining os.environ read of the old name is INSIDE tracker_dir_override. KEY: warn-once is required because tracker_dir is on a hot path AND the logger.warning leaks to stderr via Python last-resort handler — a per-call warning would corrupt CLI golden-stderr tests. AC2 (tests pass under both names): migrated the 4 test files that SET the tracker dir to REBAR_TRACKER_DIR (so the deprecation warning fires ONLY in the dedicated alias test, never in golden contexts); isolation fixtures also delenv REBAR_TRACKER_DIR for symmetry; new test_config_tracker_dir.py covers canonical/alias/canonical-wins/warn-once/default/reads-resolver. Full offline suite green: 1947 passed, 9 skipped. Subagent review: correct + safe, NO critical/major (verified canonical-wins, warn-once reset, no missed sites, no subprocess handoff of the old name, reconciler import resolves, verify gate untouched; applied the minor delenv-symmetry hardening it suggested). Parent 15f6 still blocked by EV-3c (reconciler/LLM tunables + id-guard value-flip).
